definition: The Bernanke-Gertler-Gilchrist financial accelerator amplifies credit-equity transmission by approximately 1.5-2x and is currently loaded because EBITDA addback distortion means true leverage is higher than model calibrations suggest, implying a narrower distance-to-default and higher external finance premium elasticity than observable data indicate. Both NK and Post-Keynesian frameworks converge on this conclusion.
evidence: Embedded in Fed DSGE models (FRB/US, SIGMA)
EBITDA addback distortion means accelerator is more loaded than baseline calibrations suggest
True leverage of 7.5-8.5x implies narrower distance-to-default
Both NK and Post-Keynesian frameworks arrive at same conclusion: feedback loop is primed
open_questions: What is the true accelerator coefficient with addback-adjusted leverage?
Can the amplification factor be directly measured rather than calibrated?
